title: `Error validating CNI config file` and network errors in rootful podman
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

The default `podman` network for rootful podman is broken. It constantly prints warnings to stdout about errors while validating the CNI config file and won't launch containers. When I move the file `/etc/cni/net.d/87-podman.conflist` somewhere else it complains that it cannot find certain plugin types.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `sudo podman network ls`

**Describe the results you received:**

For `sudo podman network ls`:
```
$ sudo podman network ls
WARN[0000] Error validating CNI config file /etc/cni/net.d/87-podman.conflist: [failed to find plugin "bridge" in pat
h [/usr/local/libexec/cni /usr/libexec/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /usr/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin] failed to find plugin "portmap" in path [/usr/local/libexec/cni /usr/libexec/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /usr/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin] failed to find plugin "firewall" in path [/usr/local/libexec/cni /usr/libexec/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /usr/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin] failed to find plugin "tuning" in path [/usr/local/libexec/cni /usr/libexec/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /usr/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin]]
NETWORK ID    NAME        DRIVER
2f259bab93aa  podman      bridge
```

When running a container with `sudo podman run --rm -it fedora:36`:
```
sudo podman run --rm -it fedora:36
WARN[0000] Error validating CNI config file /etc/cni/net.d/87-podman.conflist: [failed to find plugin "bridge" in pat
h [/usr/local/libexec/cni /usr/libexec/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /usr/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin] failed to find plugin "portmap" in path [/usr/local/libexec/cni /usr/libexec/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /usr/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin] failed to find plugin "firewall" in path [/usr/local/libexec/cni /usr/libexec/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /usr/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin] failed to find plugin "tuning" in path [/usr/local/libexec/cni /usr/libexec/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /usr/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin]]
WARN[0000] Failed to load cached network config: network podman not found in CNI cache, falling back to loading network podman from disk
WARN[0000] 1 error occurred:
                                * plugin type="tuning" failed (delete): failed to find plugin "tuning" in path [/usr/local/libexec/cni /usr/libexec/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /usr/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin]

Error: plugin type="bridge" failed (add): failed to find plugin "bridge" in path [/usr/local/libexec/cni /usr/libexec/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /usr/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin]
```

I also noticed that the default podman network cannot be removed. After I have moved the CNI file somewhere else, the errors become different:

For `sudo podman network ls`:
```
$ podman network ls
NETWORK ID    NAME        DRIVER
2f259bab93aa  podman      bridge
```

For `sudo podman run --rm -it fedora:36`:
```
$ podman run --rm -it fedora:36
WARN[0000] Failed to load cached network config: network podman not found in CNI cache, falling back to loading network podman from disk
WARN[0000] 1 error occurred:
                                * plugin type="tuning" failed (delete): failed to find plugin "tuning" in path [/usr/local/libexec/cni /usr/libexec/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /usr/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin]

Error: plugin type="bridge" failed (add): failed to find plugin "bridge" in path [/usr/local/libexec/cni /usr/libexec/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /usr/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin]
```

Note that this issue doesn't occur when running rootless containers as regular user. Here's the content of the offending CNI config:

```
{
  "cniVersion": "0.4.0",
  "name": "podman",
  "plugins": [
    {
      "type": "bridge",
      "bridge": "cni-podman0",
      "isGateway": true,
      "ipMasq": true,
      "hairpinMode": true,
      "ipam": {
        "type": "host-local",
        "routes": [{ "dst": "0.0.0.0/0" }],
        "ranges": [
          [
            {
              "subnet": "10.88.0.0/16",
              "gateway": "10.88.0.1"
            }
          ]
        ]
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "portmap",
      "capabilities": {
        "portMappings": true
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "firewall"
    },
    {
      "type": "tuning"
    }
  ]
}
```


**Describe the results you expected:**

I would expect podman to list networks without errors. I would also expect podman to launch containers like it did before.


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.0.3
API Version:  4.0.3
Go Version:   go1.18
Built:        Fri Apr  1 20:21:54 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

  cpus: 12

  hostname: taf
  idMappings:

    uidmap:

      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
  kernel: 5.17.6-300.fc36.x86_64
  linkmode: dynamic

  memFree: 7504080896
  memTotal: 33405071360
  networkBackend: cni
  ociRuntime:
    name: crun

    path: /usr/bin/crun
    version: |-
      crun version 1.4.4
      commit: 6521fcc5806f20f6187eb933f9f45130c86da230
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +YAJL
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    path: /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock
  security:
    apparmorEnabled: false
    capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT
    rootless: true

  slirp4netns:

    version: |-
      slirp4netns version 1.2.0-beta.0
      commit: 477db14a24ff1a3de3a705e51ca2c4c1fe3dda64
      libslirp: 4.6.1
      SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 3
      libseccomp: 2.5.3
  swapFree: 8450732032
  swapTotal: 8589930496
  uptime: 1h 27m 58.64s (Approximately 0.04 days)
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


I did consult the troubleshooting guide, there wasn't an issue fitting my particular problem. I haven't tried compiling from scratch.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

- Host OS is Fedora Silverblue 36 (36.20220511.0)

comments.body:
Hi @har7an , Thanks for creating the issue.

Does you system contains CNI plugins ? what do you get when you do `which bridge` ?
I think its missing most of the container-networking plugins, you'll need to make sure all the relevant CNI plugins are present on any one of these  `/usr/local/libexec/cni /usr/libexec/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /usr/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin` or if you have plugins on a different path please configure `containers.conf` so podman could reach them see: https://github.com/containers/common/blob/main/docs/containers.conf.5.md#network-table

Hi @flouthoc, thanks for the quick response.

Here's the output of `which bridge`:
```
# which bridge
/usr/sbin/bridge
```

I have never worked with these plugins, hence I'm pretty puzzled why it stopped working after the update from F35. None of the `cni` paths you mentioned exists on my installation. If there were plugins on a different path I wouldn't know about them.

But I just dug a little deeper and it's specific to my OS (See https://github.com/fedora-silverblue/issue-tracker/issues/246). The container networking plugins have been removed in the upgrade from F35 to F36. Sorry for the noise!

For anyone else reading this: The fix is to run `podman system reset --force`. Be careful as this will wipe all of your containers, networks, etc. Taken from here: https://github.com/fedora-silverblue/issue-tracker/issues/246#issuecomment-1088929507.
